review_text:
[I received this product in exchange for my honest review] The YSL Libre Berry perfume is a beautiful feminine scent, but if you already own the original YSL Libre, I don’t think this one is different enough to fully justify buying both. They smell very similar overall, and the signature Libre DNA is definitely still the man star. But you can pick up on a few soft berry notes, slightly sweeter, more delicate femininity. which adds a nice twist. 
overall, it’s gorgeous, elegant and easy to wear, but not much of a dramatic change from the original.

review_text: [I received this product in exchange for my honest review] This fragrance definitely lives up to the hype that it’s gotten. It feels vibrant and sophisticated. It’s fruity without being cloying, which is a hard balance to strike. Instead, it feels dark, polished and expensive. The longevity is solid. I can still smell the warm vanilla base on my skin of the end of the day. It’s the perfect pick if you want something that works for the office but still has enough edge for going out. Definitely a new favorite for when I wanna feel put together without trying too hard.

review_text:
[I received this product in exchange for my honest review] Yves Saint Laurent Libre Berry Crush Eau de Parfum is a stunning fruity-floral flanker that instantly feels both playful and refined. I hadn’t tried the original Libre before this, but you can definitely sense its DNA here! The iconic orange blossom and lavender give it that clean, aromatic, sophisticated backbone.

What makes Berry Crush special is the raspberry accord, which adds a juicy, slightly sweet twist without ever tipping into overly sugary territory. The hint of coconut accord softens everything beautifully, giving it a creamy, smooth finish that feels warm and elegant rather than tropical. It’s the perfect balance of fruity and florals. Sweet, yet still grown and polished.

The quality is exactly what you’d expect from YSL: well-blended, luxurious, and long-lasting with a soft but noticeable sillage. It wears effortlessly from day to night and feels versatile enough for any season. This flanker stands confidently on its own while still honoring the original Libre DNA. Honestly, it’s making me want to try the original now.

From the gorgeous packaging to the scent itself, this perfume is beautiful in every way. Feminine, modern, and sophisticated with just the right hint of berry sweetness 🍓✨
title: Feminine, modern, and sophisticated

What stands out to me is how smooth it wears. It stays true throughout the day and never turns harsh or overly sweet, which is something I’m picky about with berry notes. There’s a richness to it that feels intentional, not trendy.

It’s memorable in a quiet way, people tend to ask what I’m wearing rather than immediately clocking it as “strong.” I would absolutely rebuy this. It feels like a well-done variation of Libre that still has its own identity, and it fits naturally into my rotation rather than competing with everything else.

review_text:
From the very first spritz, YSL Libre Berry Crush Eau de Parfum transports you into a playful, addictive world of lush berries and creamy sophistication. This fragrance is the ultimate balance between bold sweetness and elegant femininity — a scent that demands attention without trying too hard.
This perfume doesn’t just smell amazing — it sticks around. The longevity is impressive, staying vibrant for hours without fading into a whisper. The projection is just right: noticeable, captivating, and never overpowering.
YSL Libre Berry Crush is more than a fragrance — it’s a mood. It’s fun, flirty, and fabulously memorable. If you want a perfume that feels like a burst of happiness with a chic, grown-up finish, this is the one. A total sensory joyride and a must-have in anyone’s collection!

title: Beautiful
author: Jasminetran1992

review_text:
Raspberry, Coconut, Orange Blossom, Diva Lavender, and Vanilla can be found in YVES Saint Laurent Libre Berry Crush Eau De Parfum. This ties for first place for my favorite perfume. I can’t say enough good things about this bottle. 

How I discovered it was that I was at a friend’s birthday party at a bar. A woman who was a stranger whom had a bit too much to drink, came over and gave me a huge hug while she sang to me. I was embarrassed, yet I caught a whiff of a very sweet smell and it became the ice breaker to ask what it was. She stopped hugging me and told me it was Libre by YSL. She said she only put on half a squirt and thought that it was overpowering. My friends and I all talked about how much we loved it. She said she always receives compliments when wearing Libre. 

I literally drove an hour and a half to the mall to find this bottle. Since getting it, I have been as happy as I can be. It is right up my alley as far as being sweet smelling. I am hooked. 10 hours later after a spritz and I am still smelling amazing. It is strong and I love that because I feel like I’m getting my moneys worth out of every spray. As soon as I step outside, I feel like it comes alive.

The bottle is attractive too.
title: My newest Favorite Sweet Perfume
author: BluntMelissa108

review_text: [I received this product in exchange for my honest review] First off the packaging is super cute, i’m loving all of the YSL fragrances but this one is so well balanced because of the hint of berry its perfect for date night i love this scent super feminine and sensual a perfect balance and it’s long lasting i would say it’s more like an afternoon/night perfume in my opinion for when you are more dressed up

review_text:
[I received this product in exchange for my honest review] This is a great perfume that stands out among my collection. It is very fruity without being overly sweet and is very sophisticated and complex. There is also a mild floral note that's very fresh with a vanilla complement to round it all out. Not overpowering but has a great scent throw still. Wears very well throughout the day without turning sour. Overall very feminine and elegant. Bottle is also gorgeous
 Definitely recommend!

review_text: [I received this product in exchange for my honest review] If elegance was a bottle it would be this. The packaging is beautifully designed and looks perfect for fall and winter. title: Sweet like raspberries
author: jnnes

review_text: [I received this product in exchange for my honest review] As a huge fan of the Libre line, I was impressed by this new flanker. I felt like it was the first truly unique flanker to come out from the Libre line, and had a very deep and sensual undertone to the jammy berry opening. The lavender is not very strong in this one, and lends more of a subtle but sultry edge to the fragrance.  The projection is surprisingly intimate, but long lasting especially on clothes. I own the original EDP as well as the intense, and felt like this was a well-rounded interpretation that brought something new to the Libre line, instead of a new reimagining of vanilla in Libre. Overall, I was extremely impressed by the complexity and crafting of this fragrance. It has exceptional wear and I continue to think that YSL has excellent value in their fragrances for the artistry and quality of the juice and the user experience with the packaging.
title: A stunning interpretation of Libre

title: Absolutely love!
author: Mviolets26

review_text: The first spray takes me somewhere electric. I’m standing in a sunlit market just as morning turns into afternoon. Ripe raspberries crushed between my fingers, their juice sharp and tangy It’s playful and confident, it’s the kind of scent that makes me smile without thinking about it. As it settles, I’m carried south. The tart fruit melts into French lavender, clean and invigorating, like fresh air rushing through open windows on a long drive. It keeps everything polished and calm. Then orange blossom from Morocco blooms very warm, creamy, and sensual like golden light on my bare skin at sunset. This is where the berry crush starts to feel intimate, almost magnetic. Finally, it draws me somewhere softer and closer. The coconut accord wraps around everything like my skin warmed by the sun, smooth and creamy without being tropical or loud. It balances the brightness, turning the scent into something you want to stay in. This is the moment where the fragrance feels indulgent, like dessert after a perfect meal. Sweet, warm, and deeply satisfying. I wanted to evoke how I feel while wearing it through a story. There’s only one downside to my story and it’s that I wish it was more fruity. I love a fruity floral and I pick up a lot of floral from this. Other than that it’s sophisticated and elaborate. Truly brilliant. I love it! The projection is strong. The sillage left a huge trail when I got up to go to the kitchen. My son adores the scent. The longevity lasts until I go to sleep. To me the value is exceptional. The all new Libre Berry Crush Parfum is exquisite. Another lovely flanker by YSL.
title: An ode to YSL Libre Berry Crush
author: jessicaraek

review_text:
[I received this product in exchange for my honest review] Libre Berry Crush is that girl in a bottle. It’s fruity, bold, and super feminine without being childish. The berry note gives it a juicy sweetness right away, but it still has that signature Libre vibe, clean, confident, and grown.

It smells like a sweet berry perfume with a sexy, warm dry down, and it lasts so long on my skin and clothes. The projection is amazing too—people will definitely notice it, but it still feels classy and expensive.

This is perfect if you love sweet perfumes, but want something that still feels mature, luxe, and powerful.

✅ Perfect for: date night, brunch, events, going out
💖 Smells like: juicy berries + soft warmth + expensive confidence
🔥 Longevity: long-lasting with strong projection

author: bluelovee

review_text:
I received this product - Yves Saint Laurent Libre Crush Eau De Parfum from Influenster in exchange for my honest review. The YSL Libre Berry Crush surprised me in a good way. It's playful yet bold, not overly sweet, and definitely more grown-up than typical berry-forward scents. It feels like a confident twist on fruity florals, with a bit of edgy sophistication that makes it suitable for both daytime wear and evenings out.

Scent Profile
Top Notes: Bright, juicy berries - not candy+ish, more like fresh picked raspberries with a hint of citrus zest.
Heart Notes: A floral backbone (lavender and orange blossom) that balances the fruitiness and adds warmth.
Base: Soft musk and vanilla - smooth and cozy but not heavy.
The overall vibe is fruity-floral with a chic, modern edge rather than a sugar bomb. It’s feminine without being saccharine, which I appreciate.

Longevity & Projection
Longevity is good - I get a solid 6-7 hours on skin and longer on clothes. Projection is moderate, meaning it's noticeable in close to moderate social settings but not overpowering. It's the kind of scent that earns "What are you wearing?" rather than "You smell like perfume bomb."

When to Wear It
This feels versatile. My personal sweet spot for it has been:
Daytime plans - brunch, work (if you like a bit of personality in your scent)
Casual evenings - dinner dates, drinks
Spring & summer - the fruity notes sing in warmer weather
It's sexy but not overly sultry; confident but not intimidating.

Personal Takeaways
I keep reaching for this when I want something fun yet grown-up. The berry aspect never feels artificial, and the floral and musky base keeps it from feeling like a teen fragrance. I wish the longevity was a little stronger on my skin, but it does linger nicely on clothes.
If you love fragrances that feel like a modern classic with a twist, this one delivers.
title: Sophisticated berry bloom parfum
author: TRSwiftie

review_text:
[I received this product in exchange for my honest review] If you are looking for a scent that doesn’t just enter a room but commands it, look no further. The Yves Saint Laurent Libre Berry Crush is, quite simply, bottled confidence. From the moment I first spritzed this on, I knew my fragrance game had changed forever.
The Scent: A Symphony of Power and Elegance
The fragrance itself is an intoxicating masterpiece. It opens with this vibrant, juicy burst of wild berries that feels sophisticated rather than sugary. As it settles, it develops into this rich, "crushed" floral depth that feels velvety and expensive. It’s the perfect balance of "boss girl" energy and mysterious allure.
The "Head-Turning" Effect
I am not exaggerating when I say this perfume turns heads everywhere I go. Whether I’m walking through a crowded lobby, sitting in a restaurant, or just running errands, people actually stop me to ask what I’m wearing. It has this incredible projection that feels like an invisible, glamorous cape. It makes me feel seen, noticed, and undeniably chic.
Confidence in a Bottle
There is something about this specific berry-meets-luxury profile that instantly shifts your posture. When I have this on, I feel like the best version of myself—bold, radiant, and completely in control. It’s my secret weapon for big meetings or nights out when I want to feel untouchable.
The Aesthetic: A Work of Art
We have to talk about the bottle. YSL always nails the packaging, but this one is on another level. The iconic gold YSL logo wrapped around the heavy, crystalline glass is a statement piece for any vanity. It looks just as lavish as it smells—bold, gold, and unapologetically high-end.
Final Verdict: 10/10. If you want to smell like wealth, confidence, and berry-infused perfection, you need this in your collection. Be prepared for the compliments—they won’t stop!

author: tinabui0502

review_text:
[I received this product in exchange for my honest review] For those of us who are fond of Libre’s iconic floral lavender scent, the Libre Berry Crush Eau de Parfum is an enticing reinterpretation of the original Libre with an extra infusion of the alluring juiciness of raspberries. This one is absolutely amazing at bringing you into a state of indulgence. While exhilarating in its own way, this perfume gives a gentle salute to the core of Libre. 
To best describe this fragrance experience, imagine starting off with a slightly sweet raspberry note that then dries down into a warm, soft floral. It feels feminine and yet not overwhelmingly sweet. It really does hug the skin in a lovely way. 
This fragrance has an inviting vibe that will leave a noteworthy impression. It has a nice projection without being too heavy. There are characteristics of a sweet gourmand scent, while also coming off as mature and seductive. 
I have worn it consistently over the past several weeks and have received numerous comments from both friends and acquaintances to strangers in public. This stays as a long-lasting fragrance on my skin, as especially more so when combined with an unscented balm. 
Applied onto my main pulse points by gently dabbing onto the inner sides of my wrist, inner elbow and sides of my neck. This gives you a perfect amount of scent to personally enjoy the scent while having others pick up on it when they are standing close to you or when hugging.
Overall, this perfume highlights the tanginess of raspberries crushed into Libre’s warm floral signature scent, which sweetly intertwines the revitalizing French lavender and sensual Moroccan orange blossom. These base notes from France and Morocco come together like a song and dance. There is a lovely vibrance with the addition of coconut accord, which adds a factor of creaminess to effortlessly blend together the top notes. I personally appreciate that this rendition balances freshness with warmth.
title: A reinterpretation of the iconic Libre

review_text: When I first sprayed Kayali Yum Boujee Marshmallow on my skin, it didn’t immediately read like a sugary carnival treat — instead it opened with a soft creaminess that feels more like toasted marshmallow over a subtle warm breeze than a straight-up candy push. There’s a gentle milky sweetness at first, but it’s balanced by a whisper of warmth that keeps it from feeling childish. Instead of a flat sugary blob, it smells textural — like marshmallow folded into warm vanilla cream with a hint of something almost plush and cozy underneath. As the fragrance dries down, the sweetness doesn’t escape, but it becomes more refined and wearable. The heart sits in this smooth space where marshmallow meets something almost like warm almond milk and soft white musk, creating a cloud-like effect that clings close to the skin. Longevity on me was respectable — it stayed perceptible for most of the day without turning sharp or cloying, and on clothes it held a gentle airy sweetness well into the next wear. It’s comfortable, but not boring; the sweetness stays balanced and doesn’t become heavy or syrupy. What I love about this one is how approachable it feels — it’s sweet, but in a sophisticated cozy way rather than loud or juvenile. Spraying it on pulse points (inside wrists, neck, even a light mist on a scarf) gives it this soft, inviting trail that’s comforting without being overwhelming. I’d layer it with a deeper vanilla or soft musk if I wanted to anchor the sweetness for evening wear, or let it stand alone for a warm, playful daytime vibe. If you’re looking for a marshmallow fragrance that feels grown-up, this one hits that balance beautifully.

The dry down is warm, cozy and a little musky, which balances everything beautifully. I keep catching little waves of it throughout the day and it makes me smile every time. If you love warm gourmand fragrances that feel playful but still chic, this one is so worth trying. I already want a bigger bottle.

The dry down is where this fragrance truly shines. As it settles, it becomes more refined and composed. Pink musk adds balance and harmony, transforming the initial playful sweetness into an elegant, cozy skin scent. What starts youthful evolves into something polished and confidently wearable.

If you enjoy sweet fragrances but want something grown and thoughtfully blended, this one is a standout.

title: My favorite gourmand !
author: jxllyannm

review_text:
I adore this fragrance so much that I’ll have to get a full-sized bottle. Initially, when I first sprayed this, I thought it was going to be very similar to some of the perfumes out in the market, but as time went by, I found myself liking this more and more. It does have a certain element that made it stand out to me. 

This fragrance starts sweet and marshmallow-like, but the complexity that is shown in about half an hour to an hour later is what caught my attention. It doesn’t stay nauseatingly sweet throughout; it evolves into a slightly floral gourmand that gets airy. On the initial spray, I did think it came on slightly strong, but the middle and dry-down work pretty well with my skin chemistry. During hours 2 to 7, it stayed this beautiful marshmallow scent with vanilla extract notes that smelled lowkey like delicious Rice Krispies to me. I think the pink musk also keeps it grounded from being too nauseatingly sweet.

This was pretty long-lasting on me; I could still smell it 7 hours later. I know some reviewers have a different experience, but from all the Kayali fragrances I’ve tried, this is one of the three that last really long for me. The projection and sillage are pretty noticeable with this one; this creates a scent bubble on you for hours once you wear it. The only caveat is that I wish the atomizer were of slightly better quality. 

Overall, I’d give this a 5/5. This is one of my favorite winter gourmand scents.

review_text: It opens fruity and bright, then quickly turns into fluffy strawberry marshmallow and whipped vanilla. Soft, feminine, and deliciously sweet, it smells like a luxury dessert cloud. Perfect for everyday wear if you love gourmand fragrances with a cute, boujee twist.

review_text: KAYALI Yum Boujee Marshmallow is an absolute dream of a fragrance that feels fun, cozy, and luxurious all at once. From the first spray, you’re greeted with a sweet, fluffy marshmallow note that instantly lifts your mood. It smells like a soft cloud of sugar with a grown-up twist. The sweetness is balanced perfectly so it never feels overpowering. There’s a creamy warmth that makes it feel comforting and addictive. It reminds me of a fancy dessert in a chic café. The scent lingers beautifully throughout the day. I received so many compliments while wearing it. It feels playful yet sophisticated at the same time. The packaging is just as stunning as the fragrance itself. The bottle looks gorgeous on my vanity. A little goes a long way with just a few sprays. It layers wonderfully with vanilla or floral perfumes. The dry down becomes even more smooth and cozy. It’s perfect for everyday wear or special occasions. The marshmallow note feels soft instead of artificial. It gives off a warm and inviting vibe. This fragrance feels youthful but still elegant. It instantly boosts my confidence when I wear it. It’s great for fall and winter but works year-round too. The scent feels comforting like a warm hug. It’s sweet without being childish. KAYALI really created something magical with this one. I would definitely repurchase and recommend it to anyone who loves sweet, cozy fragrances.

review_text:
Bought the travel size of this and I loved it so much, I bought the full-size bottle. The packaging is lovely and you honestly don’t need to spray a lot. 

It’s such a great scent and the dry-down is an absolutely gorgeous strawberry milkshake topped with whipped cream and pink marshmallows. 

It’s a solid 9.5/10. It does not last long in the skin though. 

I would suggest spraying a tiny bit on your clothes as long as they’re not white or stain easily from alcohol so it lasts a tiny bit longer, and on your pulse points. It has a roughly 5 hour longevity. 

It has more of a synthetic strawberry smell though but in a good way. You are hit with the freesia note though when you spray it but as it dries down, it gets better and better.

review_text: Scent is sweet, youthful, playful but doesn’t last long. I have purchased the full size 3.4 oz multiple times. However, since Mona increased it from 138 to 150 I’m unsure if I will repurchase it or further support this brand. It was overpriced to begin with and lacks longevity & projection. The drastic price hike for the full size is a slap in the face for customers that have supported her and made her who she is today. Those that buy the largest size got the short end of the stick. How does that make sense. The minis stayed the same and the mid size 1.7 oz only went from $100 to $105. There is NOTHING that warranted the $12 price hike besides greed and her doing collabs with “influencers” & giving away so much PR. How have any of her loyal customers been rewarded? The new atomizers are horrible to spray(no fine dispersed mist, it just shoots out in a stream), atomizers leak, packaging is cheaper, and quality & performance have gone downhill. I have been a longtime customer of Kayali. I remember when their full size was 118 & mid size 89. Fame has gone to her head & she thinks she’s on par with designer fragrances. Ridiculous.
title: Do NOT support Mona/Kayali greed

review_text:
I initially received this perfume as a sample from a Sephora purchase (I am not one of those influencers that signed up to receive samples to review) and enjoyed it enough to order a full size.  When I first sprayed the sample I was a little confused what all the hype was about for this brand.  At first I felt that scent lacked the proper complexity for the price tag, however I  soon became addicted and started to understand.  These perfumes are designed for **LAYERING** and it hit the price point when it came to how long this scent lasted, and how great it smelled on the dry down.  The dry down is where it's at.  
  I do have a major gripe about this brand though, and it is more of a design issue--
The sample was great in delivering just the right amount of spray.  When I purchased the full size, for me, it was just a little too much spray at once.  I like this brand for LAYERING, I don't want this to be overpowering, and the full size makes it impossible to get just the right amount of spray I am seeking.
title: Good scent / Frustrating design

author: InkedGypsy81

review_text:
This fragrance is pure indulgence. Yum Boujee Marshmallow opens with a soft, airy sweetness that feels elevated rather than juvenile. The marshmallow note is creamy and smooth, balanced beautifully with warm vanilla and a subtle musky depth that keeps it from being overly sugary.

What really impressed me is the longevity for a travel spray. it lasts several hours on my skin and lingers nicely on clothing. The scent develops beautifully as it warms up, becoming more cozy and slightly sensual over time. It’s perfect for layering, but also stands out wonderfully on its own.

I love how this feels playful yet polished…sweet, feminine, and confident without being overpowering. If you enjoy gourmand fragrances that still feel luxe and well blended, this is absolutely worth adding to your collection.
title: Sweet, Luxe & Addicting

review_text:
I like creamy notes and strawberry notes, so I love this perfume. I only have a bit left now, and I like it enough that I’m going to get a full size when it’s out. My only complaint about the fragrance is that it doesn’t project as far as I’d like for as long as I’d like, but it’s not terrible or anything. You can still smell it a bit at the end of the day.

I’m giving this four stars because the travel bottle sucks. The atomizer has gotten progressively worse over the months, and at this point it just spurts droplets instead of anything resembling a mist. The lid is also pretty loose, loose enough that I’d be worried about it falling off in my bag. It’s not a very sophisticated bottle. 

I can’t vouch for whether or not the version of this that’s the same size as the travel bottle but shaped like a mini Kayali bottle is better in these aspects. But if you’re getting a mini I’d suggest spending the few extra bucks, just in case it’s better.

review_text:
Vanilla 28 is a warm, sweet perfume, with a cozy depth to it. On my skin, the amber and brown sugar really stood out, giving it a rich, slightly caramelized feel rather than a light or airy vanilla. 

It's definitely pretty and easy to wear, and it layers beautifully with other fragrances. That said, this fragrance, once on my skin, was not what I was expecting especially hence the name and the hype around it. I was anticipating a more straightforward vanilla, and this felt deeper and more amber-forward than I imagined.

Silage and longevity were moderate for me. I would also avoid spraying directly on light colored clothing since the juice is darker. 

Overall, I'd still recommend it if you enjoy warm, sweet, slightly resinous vanilla scents that work well year-round.
title: Warm, Sweet, with Rich Amber

review_text:
Probably my absolute favorite perfume. It gets me so many compliments and elevates anything I layer it with. Definitely popular for a reason and deserves the hype. 

That being said, the internet is known to market things incorrectly. Before buying this I was under the assumption this would be a cupcake vanilla frosting-esque  gourmand scent (think dulce by rosie jane). If that is what you are looking for, that is not this. This is a boozy sexy vanilla scent especially upon first spray which is what trips people up. The dry down on my skin however, in my opinion, leans more vanilla extract. Again, not vanilla frosting cupcake ice cream, but vanilla extract if it had a booziness to it. That’s the best way to describe it. I’ve gone through 3 travel sprays/minis and finally pulled the plug on a full size. I love it!

But decided to give it another try. The nasty musky alcoholic vibe totally softened and became a warm sweet spicy vanilla… a real head turner now. author: AliyahJewel245

review_text:
I had a bottle of Vanilla 28 that had matured nicely over a few months. I gave it to my daughter because I also have Bianco Latte and Tihota. V28 is lighter than BL and Tihota. V28 is a sweet, kind of rummy vanilla. BL is thick and carmel-y. Tihota is closer to V28, but a bit richer. 

I was kind of missing V28, so on a recent Sephora run I tested V28 to confirm that I still liked it. I sprayed my neck, jacket, and hair. It lasted two days on me, especially my hair. It's definitely sweet, but again, lighter than a lot of vanillas out there. It's not floral, which is a must for me. It does not have that plastic-y note, which I do get with BL. 

 For future purchases, because of the price difference, and because they are so similar, I'll chose V28 over Tihota. Because Bianco Latte has that kind of burnt rubber, plastic-y note on me, I'll chose V28 instead. 

Overall, Vanilla 28 is good choice for a pretty straight forward vanilla. It stands on it own and layers well with other fragrances. I've layered it with Invite Only Amber and Lovefest Burning Cherry with nice results.
title: Sweet Light Vanilla

review_text: PSA: For the people confused as to why different bottles of Vanilla 28 are different shades, thats because vanilla is a fragrant compound that wants to turn brown which is called maceration, as the brown deepens it releases compounds that also deepen the scent profile which can be a good thing depending on the person. Some people will put thier perfume up and wait for it to darken as it enhances the strength and sweetness of the perfume, so no your perfume has not gone off, its normal. Anyhow I Purchased the smaller bottle and I'm glad I did it was a bit pungent, with a bit of vanilla sweetness. Layering it with another Kayali scent helped a bit oncethe dry down happened. For some it works but I truly wanted to love it.  & I absolutely love gourmand scents as well! Used it a couple times and it now sits on my vanity.

review_text:
This fragrance is a beautiful balance of gourmand sweetness and subtle spice. I love the depth it carries; it feels indulgent yet refined, and I honestly can’t get enough of it. Even in my smell-sensitive office, no one has ever complained. With just a couple of spritzes, it stays close to me, creating a soft aura that feels personal and never overwhelming.

It’s warm, comforting, and elegant, the kind of scent that feels like a quiet confidence rather than something shouting for attention. Some people label it as “old lady–like,” but at 39, I find it perfectly suited to my age and style. To me, it’s timeless, sophisticated, and a fragrance that embraces maturity without ever feeling dated.

Docking 1 star because of longevity.

review_text:
Kayali Vanilla 28 is a fragrance lover’s dream—especially for those who adore warm, gourmand scents with a sensual twist. From the very first spray, you’re greeted with a rich, creamy blend of vanilla orchids and sweet tonka bean that feels instantly cozy and indulgent.

The opening is soft and slightly floral, but it quickly melts into a warm, caramelized vanilla that lingers beautifully on the skin. Hints of brown sugar and amber create a seductive depth, giving it an almost edible quality without being overpowering. It’s sweet but sophisticated, with just enough musk and patchouli in the base to keep it grounded and balanced.

author: g42864

review_text:
Kayali Vanilla | 28
Out of the entire Kayali lineup, this is the one that truly captures my heart. While there are a few strong contenders, Vanilla | 28 takes the crown with ease. It’s a rich, boozy, syrupy vanilla dream—decadent and comforting, like brown sugar and vanilla cupcakes paired with a scoop of boozy vanilla ice cream.

From the very first spray, you’re met with a beautiful blend of sweet jasmine and warm tonka bean, creating a creamy, slightly floral opening that feels both inviting and elegant. As it begins to settle on the skin, the brown sugar and vanilla notes really come alive—intertwining with the opening and creating a warm, dessert-like aura that is absolutely intoxicating.

But what truly sets this fragrance apart is its depth and balance. As it dries down, notes of amber, musk, and amberwood step in, grounding the sweetness and adding a sensual, skin-like warmth. These base notes prevent it from becoming too cloying, lending the scent a smooth sophistication that lingers beautifully.

There is a touch of patchouli listed in the notes, but it’s incredibly subtle—more of a whisper that adds a faint spice rather than taking center stage. The overall effect is magical. With incredible projection and staying power, Vanilla | 28 is a must-smell for any gourmand lover. It’s a cozy, sweet, and seductive signature scent that wraps around you like a warm hug.
title: A One of a Kind Vanilla

review_text: I am a VERY picky person when it comes to fragrances. I've worn Glossier You for 5 years now because it was the only scent that didn't trigger my asthma. That changed when I got this bad boy in the mail! Lately I've been in the mood to try something sweeter than the Glossier You, more vanilla-y. I first looked to Sol de Janeiro, trying 68 and 62, and both triggered my asthma HORRIBLY, besides just not smelling very good to me. I almost had to convince myself to like them. After those extreme disappointments, I remember bracing myself for the first spray of Vanilla Skin...and then immediately thinking "YES!!!" And I didn't have an asthma attack after impulsively spraying it all over myself! A miracle! I took a chance on this because of the inclusion of "skin" in the name and because I really liked the notes listed, which are very unique for a vanilla scent. I welcome the pink pepper note, as you could've guessed by my love for You. Once I set out to find something, it's really hard for me to be satisfied that I have found the BEST option...but I literally haven't searched for or thought about another scent after finding this. My You is rotting in my bathroom. Congratulations Phlur, you pleased one of the grumpiest and cynical fragrance consumers alive.
title: NEW FAVORITE for a picky girl!

author: AveryGib

review_text:
I recently tried PHLUR's Vanilla Skin, and let me tell you, it was a wild ride! 
Instead of the warm, sweet vanilla I was hoping for, I was hit with a wall of sandalwood and musk so strong that I felt like I’d walked into a 1960s commune. Honestly, I now smell like a 65-year-old hippy trying desperately to reclaim their youth.

I swear, this scent should come with a warning: “For the tired middle-aged woman who’s given up on life!”

I heard it called a stripper perfume, but reviews failed to mention it's only if that stripper has long since traded in her heels for orthopedic shoes. 

If you’re looking for something that whispers “sweet and alluring,” this isn’t it. Instead, you’ll smell like “I might just crochet your cat a sweater while discussing the various acid trips taken with random guys and long lost loves.”

Instead of cozy vanilla bliss, I got a scent that screams “patchouli power!” If you're aiming for “wise elder” vibes at a middle school dance, then this is your fragrance. Otherwise, save yourself the trouble and skip this one!
title: this scent should come with a warning

review_text: This is not a review of the perfume itself, but of Sephora. This is my favorite perfume and I've ordered it several times from various retailers in various sizes. I purchased this mini set online a couple of days ago and had it shipped, and was shocked when I opened the package and the color of the perfume was more amber/yellow than the light pink I'm used to, and the fragrance was also off. I reached out to Sephora customer service and they sent me a replacement, but the bottles in the second package were also the same color, so it's not a random fluke. I reached out to Prada directly and sent them a picture of my bottles from Sephora to ask if there was a change in the formula/color, and they said no, its supposed to be a light pink. They said this must mean Sephora is storing these at high temperatures and/or exposing to light that affect the color and fragrance, which is a shame that its affecting the quality of the Prada/paradoxe brand. I'm going to buy these directly from Prada instead moving forward.
title: Sephora Storage Issue Affecting Quality

review_text:
Today I am reviewing the latest addition to Prada's fragrance line, the Paradoxe Intense which I got from Influenster. This new rendition elevates the beloved original to new heights, maintaining its iconic triangular flacon while intensifying the scent's complexity and allure.

Prada Paradoxe Intense elevates the original scent to new heights with intensified floral, warm, and woody notes. The Jasmine superinfusion, Ambrofix accord, and bold Moss base create a captivating, sensual fragrance. It boasts impressive sillage and projection, making a bold statement. While the deeper, edgier character may risk overindulgence, the meticulous craftsmanship shines through. This fragrance is a must-try for those who appreciate a daring yet familiar scent, perfect for leaving a lasting impression.

review_text:
Scent Complexity:
PARADOXE by Prada is a wonderfully complex fragrance. It starts with a burst of vibrant citrus that quickly gives way to a sophisticated floral heart. As the day progresses, you'll notice warmer, woody notes emerging, creating a rich, multifaceted experience that keeps evolving. It's the kind of scent that keeps you intrigued, always revealing something new.

*Packaging Design:*  
The bottle design of PARADOXE is pure Prada – elegant and modern. Its clean lines and minimalist look are elevated by luxurious details like the gold cap and the subtly engraved Prada logo. It's not just a container for the fragrance; it's a beautiful object you'll be proud to display on your vanity.

*Projection:*  
The projection of PARADOXE is impressive. Just a few sprays are enough to make a statement without being overwhelming. It's perfect for leaving a lasting impression, whether you're at a meeting or out for dinner.

*Sillage:*  
PARADOXE has a moderate to strong sillage, meaning it leaves a pleasant, lingering trail that draws attention in a good way. It's noticeable but not overpowering, ideal for those who like their presence to be felt without dominating the room.

*Versatility:*  
One of the standout qualities of PARADOXE is its versatility. It works for any occasion, from formal events to casual outings. Its complex profile makes it suitable for both day and night, and its sophisticated charm means it fits well in any season. It's a true all-rounder in your fragrance collection.

review_text: I recently received a sample of Prada Paradoxes Eau de Parfum thanks to Macy's and Popsugar Dabble free of charge so that I may sample the Parfum and in return give my honest review. Prada Paradoxe was created by Nadège Le Garlantezec, Antoine Maisondieu and Shyamala Maisondieu. Prada Paradoxe is an Amber Floral Fragrance for Women and was released in 2022. The perfume bottle is not like any perfume bottle we have seen yet and looks very elegant. It contains The Paradoxe Parfum which is a beautiful Barbie Pink fluid fills up an upside down glass triangle with a bold black rectangular top labeled Prada. The Bottle is also beautifully Branded with the logo Prada Milano in black lettering. Prada did an excellent job designing such a beautiful and sophisticated perfume and bottle. The top notes of Prada Parafoxe are Pear, Tangerine and Bergamot; middle notes are Orange Blossom, Neroli Essence, Neroli and Jasmine Sambac; base notes are Bourbon Vanilla, White Musk, Amber and Benzoin. Once on your skin you will be left with a sweet amber scent which has a pretty good longevity lasting for 3-5 hours. I believe this perfume could be used as your daily perfume or it could be used for that special occasion. After sampling this perfume my significant other could not stop smelling me and telling how great I smell. I absolutely love this perfume and would definitely recommend it.
title: Love it

review_text: PRADA PARADOXE EAU DE PARFUM is an intriguing and unique fragrance that captivated my senses. In my opinion, it exudes a sense of mystery and sophistication with its blend of floral and woody notes ,while the heart notes of tonka bean and vanilla add warmth and depth. . The initial burst of tuberose and ylang-ylang is intoxicating, while the underlying hints of patchouli and vanilla add depth. However, the longevity could be improved, as it tends to fade relatively quickly. Overall, it's a scent for those who appreciate unconventional fragrances and enjoy making a statement.I absolutely adore PRADA PARADOXE EAU DE PARFUM! It's a versatile scent that's perfect for both daytime wear and special occasions. The sleek, minimalist bottle adds to its overall appeal. Prada paradoxe will be my favourite perfume from now on!

review_text: I ordered this perfume online after smelling one of those little magazine samples, where it seemed to be floral without being overly sweet, but in-person, it's exactly the opposite. To me, this smells exactly like an old Bath and Bodyworks scent, Forever Sunshine: bright, summery, and extremely sweet. I loved Forever Sunshine in high school, but wearing Paradoxe today, the sweetness feels overpowering. It's also a bit similar to YSL's Libre Intense (which I have and love), but even sweeter and less floral; Paradoxe isn't as rich or deep, and Libre Intense has a honey-ish sweetness whereas this smells more sugary. Overall, I don't like it because I wanted something more subtle, more distinctive, and less sweet, but people who love sugary summertime scents will probably love Paradoxe. In terms of positives, the staying power is amazing, and the bottle is beautiful: unique, heavy, and the spritzer works so well. I wish I loved the perfume half as much as I love the bottle!

author: ItsBeccaLily

review_text:
Valentino Donna Born in Roma Purple Melancholia is, without question, a stunning addition to the Born in Roma lineup. Valentino truly understood the assignment with this one. It feels romantic, moody, and luminous all at once — like twilight in Rome with silk brushing against warm skin.

On first spray, there’s an immediate elegance. The fragrance opens with a juicy brightness that feels vibrant but not loud. As it develops, the floral heart takes center stage — plush, slightly creamy, and sophisticated rather than overly sweet. There’s a velvety depth that gives it that “purple” aura — rich, sensual, and polished. The base settles into something smooth and modern, with enough warmth to make it feel dressed-up without being heavy.

Unfortunately, as beautiful as it is in the air and on paper, it simply doesn’t cooperate with my skin chemistry. On me, the balance shifts in a way that flattens some of the nuance and magic I can clearly smell otherwise. It’s one of those frustrating moments where you recognize quality, craftsmanship, and artistry — but your skin has other plans.

That said, this is objectively a job well done by Valentino. The composition feels intentional, cohesive, and luxurious. If your skin embraces it the way it’s meant to bloom, I can easily see this being someone’s signature evening scent — elegant, feminine, and quietly captivating.

Just because it doesn’t work on me doesn’t mean it isn’t beautiful. Sometimes fragrance is chemistry — and this one deserves appreciation for the artistry alone.
title: A Beautiful Creation That Didn’t Love My Skin

author: milyjc

review_text: [I received this product in exchange for my honest review] Purple Melancholia by Valentino is a fragrance unlike any other that I’ve smelled in recent memory. It’s rather light yet long lasting. The bottle is beautiful studded glass and is a gorgeous addition to my collection. The primary notes of the fragrance are Mirabelle plum, jasmine absolute, and Madagascar vanilla. To me, the most noticeable of these notes is the plum but its subtle and sneaks up on you. A soft, clean floral- slightly soapy—with an undertone of juicy, ripe plum. Emphasis on ripe - like sun warmed fruit on a hot day. It’s absolutely lovely and very wearable for any occasion. Unfortunately I don’t like how it smells on me but love it on other people. So while I wouldn’t buy it again, I absolutely recommend it to others.

review_text: [I received this product in exchange for my honest review] Purple Melancholia is a bold yet elegant fragrance that feels both modern and romantic. From the first spray, it opens a juicy, slinky sweet brightness that quickly settles into a rich floral heart. The blend feels feminine without being overly sugary. There’s a creamy, smooth undertone that balances fruitiness, giving it depth and sophistication. As it dries down, the scent becomes warmer and slightly sensual, with a soft woody and musky notes that lingering beautifully on the skin. It strikes a perfect balance between playful and refined. The longevity is impressive. On my skin, it last around 6-8 hours, with the strongest projection in the first 2-3 hours. Even after it softens, the scent remains noticeable as a close to skin fragrance. On clothing, it lasts even longer, well into the next day. It’s strong enough to make a statement but not super overpowering. Purple Melancholia projects confidently at first and leaves a subtle, elegant trail. It’s noticeable in a room without being overwhelming. Ideal for both daytime wear and evening outings. As a designer fragrance from Valentino, it sits in the higher price range. However, considering the quality of the scent, performance, and packaging, the price feels justified. It has that luxurious feel that makes it worth the investment, especially if you enjoy unique fruit floral fragrances with depth. The bottle is stunning. The signature Valentino studded glass design gives it an edgy yet glamorous look. The purple hue of the bottle reflects the mood of the fragrance, romantic, mysterious, and slightly dramatic. It feels substantial and high quality in hand, making it a beautiful addition to any vanity. As it dries down, the fragrance becomes smoother and more intimate. The sweetness softens, and a warm, comforting base remains. It leaves the skin smelling clean, slightly creamy, and elegant rather than stick sweet. I also didn’t experience any irritation, and
title: Elegant Scent Throughout the Day
author: Rbyrne420

review_text:
Donna Born in Roma Purple Melancholia is a unique flanker in the DBR line up. I really like this!!
A fruity chyrpe, it has notes of plum, osmanthus & vanilla. Upon first spritz it opens with a dark, sugary plum. I didn’t immediately recognize this as a DBR flanker, which isn’t a bad thing. I think it’s most unique in the opening- fruity but dark, playful (almost smells like grapes?) yet mature. 
This scent holds its own and has great longevity on me.  It makes a great addition to the DBR line up! 
After a couple hours on my skin, the Donna Born in Roma DNA comes through. If this scent could be described in a texture it would be soft, almost velvety.  The dry down is reminiscent of DBR extradose. I don’t think this is blind buy safe but what truly is? I love it but you’ll definitely want to get your nose on it before purchasing.

title: Put-together. Bold. Feminine. Obsessed. 💜✨

review_text:
[I received this product in exchange for my honest review] Valentino has truly captured a modern, bold spirit with Valentino Donna Born in Roma Purple Melancholia Eau de Parfum. From the very first spray, this fragrance makes a statement—rich, luxurious, and unforgettable.

The wearability of this scent is outstanding. It strikes the perfect balance between sophisticated and edgy, making it suitable for a night out, a special event, or even when you want to feel effortlessly chic during the day. It’s a versatile fragrance that stands out without feeling overly heavy for most occasions.
Application-wise, a little goes a long way. With just one or two sprays, you get a beautifully rich scent that opens up and evolves over time. The blend of notes is expertly crafted—the fruity and floral elements intertwine seamlessly with the deeper, sensual base, creating a layered experience that keeps changing as you wear it. It’s complex but not overwhelming, striking the kind of harmony that makes it feel tailored to your skin.
True to its character, Purple Melancholia is strong and long-lasting. I can confidently say this fragrance stays with you all day—through work, errands, and evening plans—without needing a re-application. It projects beautifully without being overpowering, and the sillage lingers in the best way.
title: Bold, Beautiful & Long-Lasting

review_text: [I received this product in exchange for my honest review] Valentino Donna Born in Roma Purple Melancholia Eau de Parfum was one I really wanted to love because the bottle is absolutely stunning and the atomizer delivers a fine, even mist that feels luxuriously diffused on my skin without over-saturating. From the first spray, the intensity is noticeable - confident but not overpowering - opening with a juicy burst of fruity and floral notes such as black currant and jasmine. As it settles it leaves a more floral lasting scent that leans into a classic rose nuance. As it dries down, that musky vanilla base becomes more prominent, giving it a smooth, slightly powdery warmth. On my skin, longevity is impressive just like any other Valentino perfume I have received which I can easily say is around 9-12 hours with moderate projection for the first few hours! Performance wise, it’s reliable and well constructed, but personally not my style due to its traditional elegance! I gravitate towards a more fruity blend! That said, if you enjoy refined, romantic florals with solid staying power a polished presentation, this fragrance really delivers on quality!!

review_text:
[I received this product in exchange for my honest review] I wasn’t sure what to expect with Valentino Donna Born in Roma Purple Melancholia, but wow this one surprised me in the best way. The whole vibe feels soft, moody, and a little mysterious, like walking around the city at night in a cozy sweater. It has this powdery, slightly sweet floral warmth that feels emotional and comforting without being heavy.
Mood-wise, it leans dreamy and introspective rather than bright or playful. Definitely a “main character with headphones on” type of scent. It feels intimate but still noticeable.
Performance is actually really solid on my skin. I get around 7 to 8 hours, and it lingers on clothes even longer. The projection is moderate at first (people close to you will smell it) and then it settles into a soft cloud. The sillage leaves a gentle trail that’s pretty and cozy instead of loud or overwhelming.
Overall it feels classy, romantic, and a little melancholic like the name suggests. Perfect for cooler weather or nights out when you want something feminine but low-key addictive. Not a typical sweet perfume, but if you like soft, powdery, elegant scents with personality, this one is worth trying.

author: lesanay

review_text:
I received this product by valentino x Influenster in exchange for my honest opinion.

Valentino’s Purple Melancholia is pure couture energy captured in fragrance form. From the first spritz, it wraps you in this intoxicating blend of dark fruit and smoky florals that feels both mysterious and undeniably elegant. There’s a richness to it that is almost velvety, like silk against bare skin at midnight.

What I love most is how it evolves. The opening has this slightly sweet, almost juicy depth, but as it settles, it becomes warmer, smoother, and more sensual without ever feeling overpowering. It lingers beautifully on clothes and skin for hours, leaving behind the kind of trail that turns heads and invites compliments. The bottle itself is stunning.. bold, luxe, and unapologetically glamorous, just like the scent. It feels like a statement piece on my vanity.

This isn’t an everyday safe fragrance. It’s a mood. It’s confidence. It’s stepping into your power with a little mystery. If you love deep, dramatic, feminine scents that feel expensive and unforgettable, this one is absolutely worth it.
title: Velvet drama in a bottle!

author: Sty12

review_text:
Vibe: Playful and bright yet alluring and sexy. Wearing your little black dress, sipping on a blackberry mojito in a swanky bar downtown.
Notes: plum, osmanthus, vanilla
Compared to the OG Valentino Born in Roma, Purple Melancholia opens with a bold, sweet, yet slightly tart juicy plum that is both lively and bright-but slightly mysterious.
Unlike the OG, the fruity note lingers, yet slowly dries down to a more subtle floral - osmanthus - vanilla. There is a subtle booziness to it which makes it unique, without smelling like straight up alcohol. The projection is great, and the scent lasts for 8+ hours.
To me, this is the cooler, more mysterious older sister of the Born in Roma collection that doesn't smell as familiar. She exudes confidence and dark femininity, but is still very sweet on the inside.

review_text:
[I received this product in exchange for my honest review]
If you’re looking for a high-quality perfume that lasts, this is it. I noticed that while the initial spray is great, it actually smells much better after it settles on the skin for about an hour.
The notes of juicy plum and osmanthus give it a beautiful opening, but the Madagascan vanilla in the dry down is what makes it so warm, luxurious, and addictive. It has a rich, slightly mysterious feel that makes it perfect for evenings or special nights out. review_text:
Valentino Donna Melancholia definitely lasts — I could still smell it on my skin hours later without reapplying, which is a huge plus. The projection is strong at first and then it settles into something smoother and more refined.

On me, it pulled a little stronger and woodier, which might be because my pH tends to amplify fragrances. It came off more bold and mature than I usually go for. I personally gravitate toward softer, creamier, slightly sweeter scents, so this one just isn’t my typical vibe.

That being said, it’s clearly high quality and long-lasting. If you love deeper, elegant, slightly dramatic perfumes that make a statement, you would probably really enjoy this. For me, it’s more of an evening, dressed-up scent rather than everyday wear.

review_text:
[I received this product in exchange for my honest review] First, the bottle is a total vibe! That iconic Rockstud pyramid design in this deep "Melancholia Purple" with the black leather label is an absolute stunner on my dresser.  

On first spray, you get this juicy, dark plum that’s sweet but sophisticated. Then the osmanthus peeks through, adding this soft, floral layer that keeps it from being just another fruity scent. And just when you think it can’t get any better, the dry-down hits you with this rich, comforting vanilla. It’s not a foodie, baking vanilla; it’s deeper, warmer scent.   

Performance-wise, it’s strong but not overpowering. It provides a solid medium projection; like people will notice and compliment you, but it won’t clear a room. I would also say it’s perfect for date nights, special occasions, or any time you want to feel a bit more elegant. Plus, the sprayer is really nice. It gives a fine, even mist every time, no patchiness.  

Honestly, it’s a new favorite! It’s memorable, and undeniably pretty. If you want a parfum with real depth, this is the one!

Initial spray you get a burst of a juicy plum note which makes it unique from the others in the line. The plum note has a tartness to it that cuts through the sweetness of the rich vanilla which prevents it from becoming cloying. I’ve owned a couple perfumes with an osmanthus note and sometimes they can lean too leathery, but you don’t get any leather qualities here! The dry down is where I smelt some similarities to the signature born in roma DNA; with addictive notes of jasmine and a rich vanilla. On skin this lasted me about 6-7 hours, but on my clothes it lasted even longer. I have to add that wearing this in public got me the most compliments I’ve ever gotten in a day. I normally wear this whenever/wherever but it really thrives at night. This can be worn all seasons because it’s not too sweet. I truly love how unique this fragrance is from the rest of the line and it’s simply a masterpiece. Definitely worth a smell!

author: lizmerp

review_text:
Looove. This is like a spicy, more sophisticated version of SDJ 62. It also reminds me a lot of MG but less herbal and more gourmand. Rarely do I feel like caramel perfumes are done to my satisfaction, most of them are plastic-y or cloying. This is neither. It's a beauty caramel, nutty vanilla. The pistachio is subtle but you can still smell it dancing in the background. I notice it more on the dry down and that's where it reminds me of 62. 
The opening is quite spicy, but a warm, smooth spicy. The ginger note really is something special here. I've never smelled anything else like it. Longevity wise you're looking at about 5-ish hours for one wrist spritz. The more you put on (especially on clothing) the longer it lasts.
The bottle design? Probably my favorite part! I love the no cap + locking mechanism so you never have to worry about accidental sprays. I do have hand weakness due to health issues and I consider this disability accessible, which is something I've never seen in a perfume bottle design and GREATLY appreciate. Overall it's a thoughtfully designed, beautiful perfume.

review_text: I was really excited to get this parfum since these are the scents I gravitate towards. I've worn this so many times throughout the month and everyday in my travel and work trip. Not to say I'm looking for compliments, but usually if I wear a hair oil or my other perfumes at least one person compliments the scent. I have worn this so many times since purchasing and not one person has complimented it. Which made me question if anyone can even smell it. I don't smell it after I first spray it on. And thought maybe I just got used to the scent, but I would ask my boyfriend periodically if he smells it and he says he doesn't smell anything. They advertise the smell could linger up to 12 hours and the only truth to that is if you stick your nose directly onto the spot you spray and take a big whiff, you could BARLEY smell it. And mind you, I'm referring to about an hour after spraying and I mainly smell the layering balm than the actual parfum. I've even sprayed myself a few times throughout the day, but the scent just does NOT last at all. I'm super disappointed in this and would not recommend anyone to purchase it. Save your money.

Unfortunately, this is the most difficult to spray travel size perfume I have ever used. I don't know if I got a bad sprayer, but it takes a lot of force to get this to spray and some days I just give up because it is too hard. I do love the smell of the dry down and it would be great if Rare came out with an easier to use version of this travel spray.

review_text:
I just tried the new Rare Beauty perfume and honestly… I’m obsessed. The scent is soft, warm, and clean without being overpowering. It has this cozy, comforting vibe that makes you feel put together but still natural—like your own skin, just better.

The first spritz gives off a gentle mix of musk and creamy woods, and as it settles, it turns slightly floral and sweet, but in the most subtle way. It’s not too “perfumey,” which I love. It lasts all day on my skin without fading into something weird.

The packaging is also gorgeous—minimal, soft, and very Rare Beauty. It feels luxurious but still true to Selena’s whole “feel good in your skin” message.

If you like soft, comforting scents that feel fresh and feminine without being too much, you’ll fall in love with this one.

review_text:
This perfume is simply fascinating. I’m not usually into scents that are too sweet or too citrusy, but this one is perfectly balanced — it smells beautiful and lasts for days, even after showering.

What makes it truly special to me is the packaging. I live with a disability that makes it hard for me to press spray bottles, and this is the only perfume I can use completely on my own. The sprayer is so soft and effortless that I don’t need to ask anyone for help — and that means the world to me.

It’s not just a perfume, it’s independence in a bottle. I love it so much. 💜

author: IamLekshmi

review_text:
WOAH!!! It's cozy, long-lasting, and SO unique! I'm genuinely obsessed with Rare Beauty's new Eau de Parfum. From the first spritz, it wraps you in a warm, cozy scent that feels very comforting but still elevated. It opens with a soft sweetness (I definitely get the pistachio! but everyone is different) and it settles into a creamy, woody, spicy finish that lasts all day without being overpowering. 

To me, what makes this fragrance so special is how it evolves throughout the day. Sometimes, I catch more of the vanilla and spice, other times the soft murkiness stands out. It's not your typical gourmand. It feels very sophisticated, warm, and personal. I also have super sensitive skin and had zero irritation so far. The BOTTLE is also beautiful and super easy to hold and spray, clearly designed with thought and intention.

Bonus: It layers beautifully with Rare Beauty's Floral Peony Blossom layering balm (which I also love). Highly recommend if you love comforting, skin-like scents that still feel unique and luxe.

** I saw a lot of people saying it's identical to Burberry Goddess, but honestly, I disagree. Burberry Goddess has a strong lavender note that comes off sharp in my nose, while Rare Beauty eau de parfum doesn't have lavender at all.
title: A Warm Hug in a bottle!!! <3

review_text:
Carolina Herrera Good Girl Blush Eau de Parfum with Floral Vanilla has notes of Mandarin, Ylang-Ylang, and Vanilla.
I do like it, but it isn’t as strong as some of my more expensive fragrance finds that I admire. It is more on the lighter side as well, not as sweet, yet smells good still. I am just a very sweet person.

The sent lasts for a few hours on my skin and at least 10 on my clothing. I can definitely tell that it smells way better on my skin than on my clothing. The chemistry just works more efficiently. It also smells significantly sweeter on my skin than on my clothing.

After seeing the beautiful bottle of a high heel on the Influenster site, I had to go hunt for it. It was just as beautiful as I imagined. It took me a minute to figure out how to spray it once I got it in my hands and out of the box. It spritz out from the top black part. I almost broke the heel by squeezing that, but I learned quickly.
It sits beautifully on the counter as well and makes a fashion statement in a bathroom.
title: Light, a little sweet and pleasant
author: BluntMelissa108

review_text: [I received this product in exchange for my honest review] I really love the femininity of this scent. When I think of feminine perfumes, it’s honestly hard to find one that isn’t super floral, powdery, or heavy vanilla, but I seriously think Good Girl Blush does it perfectly! It’s soft, a little sweet, and not overpowering at all. The vanilla is super subtle, so it doesn’t feel too gourmand, which I really appreciate, and the florals aren’t powdery either, which is a huge plus for me. It just feels young, fresh, and pretty without trying too hard. It also wears really well on me, I’d say it lasts around six hours, and as it dries down I start noticing little different parts of the scent coming through, like it slowly changes in the nicest way and I can smell the floral more over time. The sweetness is there, but it’s light and subtle, not dessert-like, and the florals feel fresh. I definitely smell the ylang-ylang in it, and I think that’s what gives it that warm, romantic vibe without being heavy. It just feels young, elegant, and fun! I would say this scent is perfect for date night, or honestly could be a good daily signature scent as well since it’s easily layered! Would highly recommend for those who love sweet and floral scents that are not too gourmand!

[I received this product in exchange for my honest review] I tried this little sample today and wow — the scent is absolutely feminine, soft, and beautiful. Even though it’s just a small sample, the fragrance still feels elegant and noticeable in a gentle way.

The opening is fresh and bright with bergamot and bitter almond.
The middle notes of ylang-ylang and peony add a soft, romantic vibe.
And the base of vanilla and tonka bean gives a warm sweetness that lingers lightly on the skin.

It’s a light, airy fragrance, perfect for anyone who loves a clean-girl, soft-sweet scent that isn’t too strong.
Ideal for:
💗 Work
💗 Dates
💗 Everyday wear
💗 Anyone who prefers a fresh, powdery, feminine fragrance

Even from this small sample, I can tell it’s a really lovely and charming scent — definitely worth trying! ✨💕

review_text: Carolina Herrera Good Girl Blush is a fresh, floral twist on the iconic Good Girl fragrance, offering a softer, more romantic interpretation. It opens with a bright burst of bergamot and mandarin, balanced by a delicate blend of peony and ylang-ylang at the heart. The base of vanilla and tonka bean adds a subtle sweetness without overpowering the floral notes, creating a light yet sophisticated scent. Encased in the signature stiletto-shaped bottle—this time in a pastel pink—Good Girl Blush feels playful, feminine, and perfect for spring or daytime wear.
title: Soft elegance in a bottle
author: Alexxxaaa85

review_text:
Good Girl Blush by Carolina Herrera is a reinterpretation of the Good Girl fragrance, wrapped in an unmistakable aura of luxury. It comes in the brand’s signature stiletto bottle which looks so pretty on my dresser.
At first, Good Girl Blush opens with a fresh burst of citrusy floral notes. Then peony and ylang-ylang, bring a creamy richness that feels soft and feminine. Finally vanilla and upcycled rosewater, give it a sweet, powdery finish that lasts. I love that the floral notes don’t give an old lady floral vibe, more of a sophisticated and sweet experience. The notes of Tonka bean are a nice touch. 

Final Verdict:
Good Girl Blush is a must-have in any fragrance lover’s collection.
title: Carolina Herrera reincarnated in the best way

review_text:
If you’re familiar with the original Good Girl fragrance, Good Girl Blush offers a softer, more romantic twist on the iconic scent. Right from the first spritz, it feels airy and feminine, with a delicate balance of citrus and floral notes. The opening has a bright freshness, likely from bergamot, but it quickly transitions into a creamy, powdery floral heart dominated by peony and rose.

What makes Good Girl Blush stand out is its slightly gourmand quality—there’s a soft vanilla or tonka bean undertone that adds warmth and depth without being overpowering. Unlike the bolder, sexier original Good Girl, this one leans more toward elegance and subtle sensuality. It’s perfect for daytime wear, especially in spring or summer, though it still has enough depth to transition into evening.

Longevity is moderate; on my skin, it lasts around 5-6 hours, which is decent but not as strong as the original. The projection is more intimate, making it great for those who prefer a fragrance that isn’t too overpowering.

Overall, Good Girl Blush is a beautiful choice if you love soft, floral-gourmand scents with a refined, feminine touch. It’s effortlessly chic and romantic—perfect for someone who enjoys fragrances that are both delicate and sophisticated.

Not the biggest fan of the heel bottles, but I know some people love them. Overall this is a very easy to wear fragrance, and I think most women would like it.
title: Love!!!
author: Desi00

review_text:
Carolina Herrera Good Girl is truly a standout fragrance that has made a lasting impression on me. This perfume is one that leaves a positive mark with its sophisticated, yet alluring scent. One of the things I absolutely love about it is how long-lasting the fragrance is. I typically spray it in the morning, and the scent lingers until the very end of the day—sometimes even longer. This makes it perfect for those who need a fragrance that can keep up with a busy schedule.

The scent itself is incredibly pleasant, and I believe it would make an excellent everyday perfume for anyone who enjoys a mix of elegance and warmth. I immediately fell in love with it as soon as I first tried it. It perfectly matches my personality and fragrance preferences. The best part? You don’t need to use much at all. Even with just a small spray, the scent is potent enough to leave you smelling amazing throughout the day.

The fragrance is distinctly feminine, with a luxurious, almost floral powdery feel. It opens with the fresh, citrusy notes of bergamot, which gradually give way to the rich, floral essence of ylang-ylang. What really makes this perfume special is the balance between the floral notes and the smooth, comforting warmth of vanilla, creating a truly heavenly and sophisticated scent.

It's a scent that feels like a perfect blend of elegance and sensuality. Whether you’re heading to work or enjoying an evening date or night out, this perfume is versatile enough to suit both casual and formal occasions. The fragrance has just the right amount of complexity to make it unforgettable, without being overpowering.

This has quickly become one of my all-time favorite perfumes, and it is definitely on my must-have list for future purchases. I can't speak highly enough about it—I’m absolutely in love with this scent. A little goes a long way, and it truly feels like the perfect everyday fragrance for any occasion.
title: Captivating Fragrance that Lasts All Day

review_text:
I received this product for free from influenster in exchange for a honest review. Blush by Good Girl is such a beautiful, warm, and vibrant fragrance. It opens with a pop of bergamot and bitter almond, which gives it this fruity but smooth vibe—like it’s bright, but not sharp. Then the peony and ylang-ylang in the middle come through, making it feel super floral and feminine, but still soft and inviting. The base notes of vanilla and tonka bean really round it out—sweet, cozy, and just a little sensual.

It’s one of those perfumes that feels playful and elegant at the same time. The bottle is stunning, like a statement piece on your vanity. I usually go for brighter scents, and this one absolutely hits—it’s almost tasty in the way that peony and almond mix. Honestly, I wish I’d got a bigger bottle because I keep reaching for it. Totally worth the splurge if you want to treat yourself.
title: Floral and cozy

review_text:
I recently tried the Carolina Herrera Good Girl Blush Eau de Parfum, and I am absolutely in love with it! The fragrance is a beautiful blend of floral and fruity notes that feel fresh and light, yet still have a luxurious depth. The top notes of pear and grapefruit create a vibrant, uplifting opening, while the heart of jasmine and rose adds a soft and elegant touch. The dry-down is warm and sensual, with hints of musk and sandalwood that linger beautifully on the skin.

What I love most about this fragrance is its versatility. It’s perfect for both day and evening wear—bright and energizing for daytime, but with enough sophistication to carry through to the evening. The bottle design is also stunning, and the blush pink color adds a touch of elegance to any vanity.

Overall, Good Girl Blush is a refined and charming fragrance that exudes femininity and confidence. I highly recommend it for anyone looking for a modern, yet timeless scent.

review_text: I am so picky about perfumes and I can not stand most of them. I have been on the lookout for my signature scent forever, and I found it in this. This is the first time I bought the full size of any perfume because even the ones I can tolerate I don't like enough to really commit to. Every time I have shared this with a friend they love it and go buy it for themselves immediately. To me, it smells like a bubble bath at the beginning. Specifically like the strawberry bubble bath for kids that came by the gallons we all used but way more subtle and less artificial. When it dries down, it smells a little more gourmand and vanilla. One thing I don't like about a lot of the most famous perfumes is how sharp and overpowering they are. This is soft, feminine, and powdery and the floral is very subtle and bright, not at all grandma-like. Personally, I didn't think it lasted very long as I couldn't smell it after about an hour or two, and this was my only disappointment with the product. However, people always tell me I smell good and ask what I am wearing long after I think the scent has worn off so perhaps you just become nose blind to it. Lastly, this packaging is the cutest I have seen and I love the color. I think it's so fitting that the one perfume I like looks like this!

I only use one spray and it lasts all day I love that I get wafts of it throughout the day, reminding me of how pretty the scent is.
I do not feel like it's overpowering and like I can't go into shared spaces without affecting someone with scent sensitivities.
One tip for the 30ml version, you have to remove the stiletto part to get to the sprayer, I was terrified I would break it the first time and its so cute I would be so sad. Twist it don't try to pull it off!
Overall absolutely love and highly recommend!

Middle Notes: Ylang Ylang & Peony

author: anynissa

review_text:
I received this product for free from Influenster in exchange for my honest review.

Carolina Herrera Good Girl Blush Eau de Parfum is the newest addition to the Good Girl fragrance collection from this powerhouse. It comes in the most gorgeous packaging I've ever encountered: a perfectly pink stiletto bottle that's not only beautiful but also a functional addition to any desktop. You could even use it as a phone test just for fun.  

The fragrance itself is a romantic take on the original Good Girl scent. It's got a fresh, floral, peony-vanilla combo. The peony is quite clearly the star although the other notes help to elevate everything including a double dose of vanilla, two forms of ylang-ylang and upcycled rosewater. It's delicate and feminine. Plus I find even with the smallest spray that the fragrance lasts hours and isn't overpowering. I absolutely loved it and the amount of compliments I receive while wearing this perfume is unprecedented. 

Thanks Influenster for sending me this little gem. 

Fragrance Family = Floral
Key Notes = Top - bergamot mandarin
Middle - ylang ylang
Base - vanilla absolute tonka bean
title: Elegant, romantic and long-lasting...

review_text:
I was lucky to receive this perfume in exchange for my honest review courtesy of @Influenster 
First of all, the packaging is something Audrey Hepburn would endorse! The stiletto shape design is so New York vibes with a cute pink, edgy black and elegant gold colour pops. The heel coming off to reveal the sprayer is a great touch.
The scent is very floral and powdery like when you first apply. I find it lasts on the skin until the end of the day but it wears off to a vanilla like note by the end of the day, no longer floral like as when first applied. It's a scent I can wear everyday as a fresh girly flowery scent. I would not use this as a night scent for myself but as a day only scent.
I would not buy this again but I do enjoy it!! I like flowery scents but this was almost a tad too much floral, but it was still nice.

review_text: I really enjoyed the original Carolina Herrera Good Girl perfume, so I was extremely interested to try this Good Girl Blush. I actually love the design of the perfume bottles. The iconic stiletto bottle is feminine and playful. I have to admit I did not love the fact that ( on the 30 ml sized bottle at least)  the entire stiletto heel must be removed to spray the perfume. The bottle cannot stand without the heel, and I found the heel portion difficult to remove. Not a big deal, just awkward. Perfume smells different on everyone, so I always test it on my skin, AND my clothes. I actually preferred this scent on my skin, over my clothes. This scent, to me, had a very citrusy, almost bitter opening note, then I got strong floral, then finally a sweet powdery drydown with a hint of vanilla.  I love the final scent. I did notice that it does not last long on my skin. Though this will not be the scent I reach for over and over, it is very pretty and I really enjoyed wearing this fragrance. I received this to try from Influenster for my honest review.

This fragrance has great staying power, not cloying at all at first spray and my husband, who rarely comments on perfume, complimented this one.  Automatic win, lol!  The only little annoyance with this one was the bottle itself - the cap’s fit was very tight and I was sure I was going to end up breaking the thing before the first spray.  If someone was new to the Good Girl and Bad Girl bottles, they would be quite confused on how to exactly get the top off.  Very minor issue though, would recommend this fragrance no problem.

It was a little tough at first to get the lid off, I was afraid that I might snap the heel but once I got it off it was smooth sailing the next few times.

I do not have a nose like my husband who can pick out every note in a fragrance. I found the flower scent to be overpowering for my taste and wish that it had been a little softer. I find when you first apply it is too much but after about 45 minutes its a more bearable scent. I had hoped for something a little more unique with all the scents in it but find it very generic smelling and not something that stands out for me. If you like flowery, strong and fairly long lasting this one is for you.

I see myself wearing it more in the warmer months of the year and will use over time but not something that I would rush out and buy again when it is gone.

review_text:
The Glossier You Perfume Duo is the perfect balance of softness, warmth, and sophistication. It’s one of those rare scents that truly adapts to your skin, creating a unique, personal fragrance that feels effortlessly natural and comforting.
The scent opens with subtle notes of pink pepper and iris, then settles into a creamy, musky base that’s both clean and sensual. It’s not overpowering — just that “you smell amazing” kind of scent that lingers softly throughout the day.
This duo set is ideal for fragrance lovers: the full-size bottle for home and the mini version for your bag or travel. The packaging is minimal yet elegant, making it a beautiful gift for yourself or someone special.
