review_text:
When You first came out, I was instantly addicted. It captivated me like no ever fragrance ever has. It was soft, clean, fresh, and warm at the same time. There was not a day that went by that someone did not compliment me on the fragrance. I purchased bottle upon bottle while my extensive collection collected dust. 

Then, Glossier came to Sephora & Europe. 

In order to sell You internationally, Glossier had to remove the ingredient lilial from the fragrance over concerns of skin sensitivities and possible endocrine disrupting effects. Calling lilial a confirmed endocrine disruptor is not supported by current regulatory conclusions or the 2023 in-vitro data. Dose matters. In order to get these inconclusive results, the direct exposure to lilial had to surpass anything remotely reasonable. 

This change makes the new formula less strong, with a shorter projection and longevity compared to the original, and a more alcohol-forward scent, with more pronounced wood and pepper notes. 

I need to spray half a bottle on myself just to get hints of the actual fragrance, and even then it has zero longevity or projection. I repurchase a bottle once a year now in hopes that something has improved, to no avail. The newer Glossier fragrances are all much better than You and I do love them, but nothing will ever compare to the OG You. 

I have spent soooo much money trying to find anything that smells like OG You and I’ve come close, but there’s always something missing. 

Since the scent was created in collaboration with Le Labo, the best I’ve found is Another 13. The price tag is out of this world so I use it oh so sparingly; it needs to last me a lifetime. 

I am BEGGING Glossier to bring back the formulation with lilial just in the US. He k, bring it back for a limited time. I will spend my life’s savings to buy every single last bottle.
title: I will never get over losing the original

review_text:
It's a unique and skin-scent fragrance, meaning it blends well with your natural body chemistry. The scent is warm, musky, and clean with hints of iris, pink pepper, and ambrette. It's not overpowering but instead offers a comforting, second-skin feel that makes it easy to wear daily. The fragrance is versatile, appealing to both those who prefer light, fresh scents and those who enjoy something deeper but not overwhelming. Glossier You is ideal for someone looking for a fragrance that feels intimate and natural, offering just a hint of presence rather than an assertive, bold statement. The packaging is minimalistic and sleek, staying true to Glossier's signature aesthetic.

Overall, if you're looking for a subtle, all-day wear fragrance that feels uniquely yours, Glossier You is a great choice. It’s perfect for someone who doesn’t want their scent to be too noticeable but still wants to feel like they’re wearing something special.

review_text:
Glossier You is the ultimate “your skin but better” fragrance. It opens with a bright pop of pink pepper, giving it a slightly spicy yet inviting start. As it settles, the ambrette seeds add a soft, musky warmth, while iris brings a delicate powdery touch. The ambrox rounds it out with a creamy, salty-skin vibe that makes the scent feel intimate and familiar.

What makes this fragrance stand out is how it adapts to your skin, making it unique to each wearer. The minimalist bottle with its thumbprint indent feels personal, just like the scent itself. If you love warm, sheer fragrances that enhance your natural scent rather than overpower it, You is a must-try.

author: bmworthy

review_text:
This is my first Glossier perfume I’ve ever tried. I have heard so many rave reviews about this “You” fragrance, so I was so excited to give it a try! 

It does come in a plastic carton with a thick rubber band. I definitely was confused by this as I’m used to perfume coming it boxes. But the plastic carton did keep the perfume safe and didn’t break, so I was ok with it. It comes in a very minimalist ombré pink bottle. I did really like the thumb print indent on the bottle, and felt it was very unique. It made it feel really nice to hold onto and spray it. 

Now as for the scent…this smells different on everyone. Luckily for me, it came off as a beautiful scent that I really enjoyed. I can see how this can be described as a “skin enhancing” perfume, that can be layered with other perfumes. I can smell a mix of the iris, musk, pink pepper, powder, and amber in it. It’s not an overly powerful perfume, with a huge sillage. I’d say it lasted on me for roughly 4 hrs. It does last a bit longer on clothing though as I’d get stiffs of it all day. I did get tons of compliments and people asking what I was wearing, so the sillage was still enough for people to notice. To me this is a perfume you can wear day, night, in all seasons, in the workplace, and for all occasions. It’s very versatile and I’d describe it as a soft, warm, powdery, musky, floral, slightly sweet fragrance. To me it’s just a very cozy smell, and it also layered really nice with other similar fragrances. I’d definitely test it out before committing to buying a full bottle, due to it smelling different on people. It’s also a very affordable perfume at only $78.00 for the 1.7oz/50ml sized bottle, which you don’t see nowadays. I personally love this fragrance and now want to try other Glossier scents!
title: A Soft & Cozy Beautiful Scent!
author: Dena1384

review_text: Glossier You is a warm, skin-like fragrance that feels personal and intimate. It has a cozy yet sophisticated scent with creamy, slightly sweet notes- a hint of pink pepper for warmth, and a soft, clean finish that blends beautifully with your natural scent. The bottle is minimal and chic, with a smooth, weighty feel that fits comfortably in your hand. This perfume is perfect for everyday wear, whether you’re heading to work, running errands, or going out, and it lingers close to the skin without being overpowering. A few spritzes on pulse points allow the scent to develop throughout the day, lasting around 6–8 hours on the skin and even longer on fabric. It’s subtle yet addictive—the kind of fragrance that makes people stop to ask me what I’m wearing! If you love scents that feel warm, effortless, and unique, Glossier You is a must-try.

title: Such a unique scent!
author: CaseyMtz08

review_text: If there’s one fragrance I keep reaching for, it’s Glossier You. This scent is pure magic on the skin—warm, sheer, and effortlessly wearable. It’s not one of those overpowering, statement-making perfumes; instead, it blends with your natural scent, creating something unique to you. I love how it starts with a pop of pink pepper, bright and slightly spicy, before softening into that musky, cozy ambrox and ambrette seed combination. The hint of iris adds just enough powdery freshness to keep it feeling clean yet intimate. One of my favorite things about Glossier You is how it truly becomes a second skin. It doesn’t feel like you’re wearing perfume—it feels like you just naturally smell incredible. I’ve had people stop me mid-conversation to ask what I’m wearing, only to say, “It just smells like you.” That’s the magic of this scent—it’s personal, familiar, and never too much. And let’s talk about the bottle! I absolutely adore the minimalist yet sculptural design. The subtle thumbprint indent makes it feel so personal, like it was designed to fit perfectly in your hand. Plus, the fact that Glossier keeps sustainability in mind with their eco-friendly packaging makes me love it even more. If you’re someone who loves warm, musky fragrances that feel intimate and natural rather than heavy or overpowering, Glossier You is a must-try. It’s one of those perfumes that doesn’t just sit on your skin—it becomes part of you.
title: The scent that becomes you.

This was the moment of truth - I am so glad that it still gives me the same cozy, clean skin scent feeling as my OG bottle. Though I’m sure the formula has changed, I’m happy to report that I still love it just as much. As a bonus, I’m no longer so scared to use my old bottle now that I’ve started this one as well and love it so much.

review_text:
I tried the sample size of Dolce & Gabbana Light Blue Pour Homme Eau de Toilette, and I have to say—it’s a perfect everyday fragrance. The scent opens with a burst of fresh citrus that feels super clean and invigorating, then settles into a smooth mix of rosemary and patchouli that gives it a warm, masculine depth. It really captures that Mediterranean vibe—fresh, breezy, and effortlessly cool.

It’s not overpowering, but it lingers just enough to leave a subtle, classy impression. Great for daytime wear, especially in spring and summer. If you’re into clean and vibrant fragrances with a slightly sensual edge, this one’s worth checking out.
